---
title: "nmi_grad"
generated: true
description: "Closed-form normalised-mutual-information (Studholme) gradient."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.metrics.information`](../nitrix-metrics-information.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:nmi_grad -->
```python
nmi_grad(moving: Float[Array, '...'], fixed: Float[Array, '...'], *, bins: int = 32, range_moving: Optional[tuple[float, float]] = None, range_fixed: Optional[tuple[float, float]] = None, sample_stride: int = 1, eps: float = 1e-10) -> Float[Array, '...']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:nmi_grad -->
Closed-form normalised-mutual-information (Studholme) gradient.

The analytic per-voxel derivative of [`mutual_information`](../nitrix-metrics-information/mutual_information.qmd) with
`normalized=True` ($\mathrm{NMI} = (H_m + H_f) / H_{mf}$) with
respect to the moving intensities,
$\partial\mathrm{NMI}/\partial\mathrm{moving}$, the normalised
analogue of [`mi_grad`](../nitrix-metrics-information/mi_grad.qmd) (the quotient-rule form). As in the Mattes
derivation, the gradient flows through only the moving soft-bin weights, so
the fixed marginal entropy $H_f$ is invariant
($\sum_a \partial\beta_m/\partial m = 0$). The quotient rule over
$H_m$ (which varies) and $H_{mf}$ then gives

$$
\frac{\partial\mathrm{NMI}}{\partial m}(x)
= -\frac{s_m}{N\,H_{mf}}\left[D_m[k]
  - \mathrm{NMI}\left((1 - t_f)\, D_{mf}[k,l]
  + t_f\, D_{mf}[k,l+1]\right)\right]
$$

with $s_m = (\mathrm{bins} - 1) / \mathrm{span}_m$; $k$ and
$l$ the moving and fixed lower bins; $t_f$ the fixed fractional
weight; $D_m[a] = \log P_m[a+1] - \log P_m[a]$ the moving-marginal
forward difference; and $D_{mf}[a,b] = \log P[a+1,b] - \log P[a,b]$
the joint forward difference (along the moving axis). Setting
$\mathrm{NMI} \to 1$ and the prefactor
$-1/(N\,H_{mf}) \to 1/N$ recovers the same $D_m$ / $D_{mf}$
structure to which [`mi_grad`](../nitrix-metrics-information/mi_grad.qmd) reduces
$\mathrm{MI} = H_m + H_f - H_{mf}$.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:nmi_grad -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `moving` | `Float` | `...` | — | Images of identical shape. |
| `fixed` | `Float` | `...` | — | Images of identical shape. |
| `bins` | `int` | — | `32` | Bins per axis (must match the cost; default 32). |
| `range_moving` | `Optional[tuple[float, float]]` | — | `None` | Optional `(lo, hi)` intensity ranges. **Pin** them for a stationary objective, as for [`mi_grad`](../nitrix-metrics-information/mi_grad.qmd). `None` (default) uses the per-image data min / max. |
| `range_fixed` | `Optional[tuple[float, float]]` | — | `None` | Optional `(lo, hi)` intensity ranges. **Pin** them for a stationary objective, as for [`mi_grad`](../nitrix-metrics-information/mi_grad.qmd). `None` (default) uses the per-image data min / max. |
| `sample_stride` | `int` | — | `1` | Estimate the joint PDF from every `sample_stride`-th voxel (ITK "Regular" sampling); the gradient is still applied densely. `1` (default) is the exact full histogram. Jit-static (a Python int). |
| `eps` | `float` | — | `1e-10` | Small constant guarding the logarithms and the $H_{mf}$ denominators (default `1e-10`). |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `...`. The gradient $\partial\mathrm{NMI}/\partial\mathrm{moving}$ (an ascent direction, as normalised mutual information is a similarity), same shape as `moving`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:nmi_grad -->
::: {.callout-note}
The empty-bin `where(... > 0, ...)` masking matches the
`normalized=True` branch of [`mutual_information`](../nitrix-metrics-information/mutual_information.qmd), so
[`nmi_grad`](../nitrix-metrics-information/nmi_grad.qmd) matches `jax.grad` of `mutual_information` with
`normalized=True` to tolerance on populated bins (the parity oracle); the
boundary divergence at an exactly-empty bin is the same documented
non-smoothness as [`mi_grad`](../nitrix-metrics-information/mi_grad.qmd). Voxels outside `[lo_m, hi_m]` get zero
force.
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:nmi_grad -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
